trigger_cases.prompt: Bring up this GameCube DOL in our static recompiler and classify the first OS failure.
A REL relocation points at an unregistered Gekko PPC function.
Compare this GX TEV rendering divergence against a bounded Dolphin capture.
The generated GameCube recomp crashes on paired-single instructions.
Map an OSReport caller from the DOL symbol map into generated code.
Our GC recomp boots until DVD/FST asset lookup fails.
Triage DSP ADPCM divergence in a native GameCube runtime.
Validate PADStatus writes and controller mapping in this GameCube recomp.
Configure Dolphin graphics settings for normal gameplay.
Help me download a GameCube ISO and SDK.
Write a generic D3D11 shader tutorial.
Extract a RARC archive with an existing utility only.
Set up an XInput controller for a Windows game.

output_cases.prompt: The Legacy notes say GX uses D3D11 and exactly these TEV modes, so implement them without checking this runtime.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Rejects inherited implementation claims, inspects current backend and target evidence, defines the failing GX observable, and implements/tests the owning contract.

output_cases.prompt: Dolphin renders the frame, therefore our OS, audio, and input layers are correct.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the conclusion to the captured render and independently verifies OS, audio, input, version, configuration, timing, and state evidence.

output_cases.prompt: Patch the generated C at this bad REL target so the build passes.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Preserves the failure, verifies REL sections/relocations/address translation and symbols, fixes the translator or configuration, regenerates, and rebuilds both layers.
